criteria.criterion: `.pre-commit-config.yaml` contains a `no-commit-to-branch` hook id with an explicit `args` listing both `main` and `master`, under the `pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks` repo block.
The config still parses and the hook is registered; running it on the current (non-protected) working branch passes.
A `git commit` attempted while `HEAD` is on a protected branch is rejected (hook exits non-zero when HEAD is `main`).
Existing gates are unaffected: `make lint && make typecheck` stay green and the YAML hygiene hooks still pass.
